title: haloragis exalata var. laevis
intro: Haloragis exalata var. laevis, also known as the sticky rātā or sticky water dock, is a plant species that belongs to the Haloragaceae family, typically found in New Zealand and Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Haloragis exalata var. laevis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
